camera_projection_measurement_valid.reason: The analysis used the wrong sign for HUGSIM's stored box-height coordinate, placing the projected mask below the rendered actor.

target_rgb_support_monotonicity.reason: All 37 reference-support frames were unavailable because the invalid projected mask had zero intersection with the rendered target; this is not evidence that HUGSIM visibility failed.

paired_actor_state_holds.expected: Actor roles, states, declared offsets, ego trajectory, actions, plans, and timestamps remain paired across the six factorial conditions.
paired_actor_state_holds.observed: All role-state comparisons have zero maximum box difference; declared planar-offset residual is zero and minimum actor footprint clearance is 0.424 m.
paired_actor_state_holds.implication: The world-state intervention was executed as declared, independently of the failed camera projection measurement.

stored_height_sign_invalidates_projected.expected: The projected target box overlaps the target-only RGB change support in CAM_FRONT.
stored_height_sign_invalidates_projected.observed: The full image contains 1641 to 3883 changed pixels per frame, but all 37 projected masks have zero intersection because their vertical locations are below the rendered target.
stored_height_sign_invalidates_projected.implication: CF-O 001 cannot adjudicate camera-space occlusion or target RGB support; a preregistered coordinate-sign correction is required.

claim_boundary: CF-O 001 accepts only paired world-state intervention integrity; its camera projection and RGB visibility measurement chain is invalidated by a diagnosed coordinate-sign error.
